item.title: Five key takeaways from the Supreme Court's landmark decision against Trump's tariffs
item.description: What's far less certain is the longer-reaching impacts as the economy and markets again adjust to a changing landscape.

item.title: With Trump's 'reciprocal' tariffs struck down, here are the industries still facing higher rates
item.description: President Donald Trump's "reciprocal" tariffs were deemed unconstitutional by the Supreme Court, but some sector-specific tariffs remain in place.

item.title: Fourth-quarter U.S. GDP up just 1.4%, badly missing estimate; inflation firms at 3%
item.description: The core PCE price index was expected to increase 3% from a year ago in December. GDP was projected to rise at a 2.5% pace in Q4.

item.title: Tariff revenue soars more than 300% as U.S. awaits Supreme Court decision
item.description: The U.S. government in January ran up a smaller deficit than a year ago, while tariff collections surged.

item.title: U.S. trade deficit totaled $901 billion in 2025, barely budging despite Trump's tariffs
item.description: The U.S. trade deficit swelled in December, closing out a year in which the imbalance was essentially unchanged.
